source.text: Honey never spoils. Archaeologists have opened 3,000-year-old jars from Egyptian tombs and found honey still safe to eat. The reason is chemistry: honey is roughly 80% sugar and only 18% water — too dry for bacteria to grow. Bees also add an enzyme that produces hydrogen peroxide, a natural preservative. As long as it's sealed, your honey could outlive you. So next time you find a forgotten jar in the back of the cupboard — you don't have to throw it out.
